info.title: Hostyt Proxy Gateway API
info.description:
## Introduction

Hostyt Proxy Gateway (HPG) is a self-hosted control panel for a fleet of Caddy reverse-proxy nodes.
Customers receive a VPS with a fixed backend IP and an allowed port range (e.g. `30000-30019`).
Through this API they map their own domains to those ports; Caddy config is pushed automatically.

## Authentication

Most endpoints require a **Bearer API key** issued from `/admin/api-keys` (admin) or `/app/api-keys` (client).

```
Authorization: Bearer hpg_live_xxxx...
```

**Node-agent endpoints** use a per-node agent token stored as a SHA-256 hash in `caddy_nodes.agent_token_hash`.
Pass it the same way: `Authorization: Bearer <token>` or `?node_token=<token>`.

**WireGuard bootstrap endpoints** are authenticated by a single-shot 192-hex-char token passed as `?token=`.
Each token has a 24 h TTL and is invalidated after first use.

## Roles

| Role    | Can do |
|---------|--------|
| `admin` | All operations |
| `client`| Read own services/routes, create/delete own routes |

`backend_ip` is admin-only — clients can never set or change it.
Allowed ports come from the service record; the server validates membership.

## Rate limits

All authenticated API endpoints share a per-key quota enforced in Redis.
Bootstrap endpoints are rate-limited to 10 req/min per IP.
Exceeding any limit returns `429 Too Many Requests`.

## Async operations

Caddy config changes (route create/delete, resync) are pushed in the background.
SSL certificate provisioning is asynchronous — poll `GET /api/v1/services/{id}/routes` and watch `status`.

tags.name: Public
tags.description: Health and version checks — no auth required.

tags.name: Customer Tunnels
tags.description: WireGuard tunnel bootstrap. Auth: single-shot `?token=` (192 hex chars). Rate-limited 10 req/min per IP.

tags.name: Node Agent
tags.description: Called by `hpg-node-agent` on each Caddy node. Auth: `Authorization: Bearer <agent_token>` or `?node_token=`.

tags.name: Node Join
tags.description: One-shot node registration. The `hpg_join_` token in the request body IS the credential — no Bearer key needed.

tags.name: Services
tags.description: Provision and manage customer services (backend IP + allowed port range). Write operations are admin-only; clients can read their own.

tags.name: Routes
tags.description: Domain → upstream-port mappings. Caddy config is pushed asynchronously. DNS verification and SSL are queued background jobs.

tags.name: Node Pools
tags.description: Node group (pool) management — admin-only. Groups determine where plans place routes.

tags.name: Plans
tags.description: Plan management — admin-only. Plans control per-client quotas and feature flags.

tags.name: Clients
tags.description: Client account management — admin-only. Creates user + client records together.

nodeAuthToken.description: Per-node agent token. Also accepted as `?node_token=<token>`.

WgPeerStats.description: Per-peer stats parsed from `wg show <iface> dump`.

last_handshake.description: Unix epoch of last WireGuard handshake

NodeDiagnostics.description: Node-level forwarding diagnostics. All fields are optional — older agents omit them.

wstunnel_healthy.description: True when wstunnel sidecar is running and bridge→host iptables rule accepted. Triggers WSS route resync on any transition.

get.description: Returns a ready-to-use WireGuard config file. The token is single-shot (24 h TTL) — consuming it marks it used.

403.description: Token invalid, expired, or already consumed

get.description:
Returns a bash script that installs `wireguard-tools`, fetches `/api/wg/bootstrap`, and creates a systemd unit. Designed for `curl … | sudo bash`. Pass `-- remove` to tear down an existing installation.

```bash
# Install
curl 'https://proxy.example.com/api/wg/install.sh?token=<token>' | sudo bash

# Remove
curl 'https://proxy.example.com/api/wg/install.sh?token=<token>' | sudo bash -s -- remove
```

parameters.name: token
parameters.description: Bootstrap token — will be consumed by the script when it calls `/api/wg/bootstrap`.

get.description: Returns whether the peer has an active WireGuard handshake. Polled by the panel UI for a live connection indicator.
Called by `hpg-node-agent` every ~30 s. Returns the full list of customer WireGuard peers the node should configure.

parameters.name: node_token
parameters.description: Alternative to `Authorization: Bearer`. One of the two must be present.

post.description: Agent posts latest handshake timestamps. Superseded by `/api/node/wg/stats` but kept live for rolling upgrades.

Full stats report from `wg show <iface> dump` — supersedes `/api/node/wg/handshakes`. Includes rx/tx byte counters, endpoint, and optional node-level forwarding diagnostics. Panel stores reset-safe byte deltas and updates `wstunnel_healthy`, triggering a Caddy route resync on any health transition.
One-shot node registration. The `hpg_join_` token in the body IS the credential — no Bearer API key needed. Returns bootstrap payload consumed by the node-agent install script.

wireguard.description: WireGuard interface config for the node

post.description: Admin-only. Links a client to a backend IP and port range. `backend_ip` can never be set by the client.

400.description: Validation error (invalid IP, port range, missing fields)

get.description: Admins can fetch any service; clients may only fetch their own.

patch.description: Admin-only. Update `status`, `external_reference`, or `notes`. Omit fields to leave unchanged.

post.description: Admin-only. Replaces the port range for a service. Existing routes using ports outside the new range must be deleted first.

get.description: Lists all routes attached to the service. Use `status` to track SSL provisioning progress. Clients can only query their own services.
Admin gets all routes; clients get only routes belonging to their own services. Optional `service_id` filter.

post.description: Maps a domain (+ optional path prefix) to an upstream port on the service's backend. `upstream_port` must be within `[allowed_port_start, allowed_port_end]`. Caddy config is pushed asynchronously; SSL certificate provisioning may take up to a few minutes.

path_prefix.description: Optional — enables sub-path routing on a shared domain

ssl.description: Enable automatic HTTPS via Caddy On-Demand TLS

400.description: Invalid domain or port out of allowed range

409.description: Domain already mapped, no node available, or plan domain limit reached

get.description: Returns a single route. Clients can only fetch routes belonging to their own services.

patch.description: Admin-only. Updates route settings. Caddy config is resynced asynchronously after the update.

delete.description: Removes the route and pushes updated Caddy config. Clients may only delete routes on services they own.

post.description: Enqueues a background DNS check. Caddy issues a certificate once DNS resolves to the node's IP.
Alias of `verify-dns`. Triggers a fresh DNS check and Caddy certificate retry.

get.description: Admin-only. Returns all nodes ordered by priority.

post.description: Admin-only. Registers a Caddy node manually (alternative to the node-join flow).
Admin-only. Rebuilds and pushes the full Caddy route config from the DB to the node. Use after manual DB edits or to recover from drift.

get.description: Admin-only. Returns full node detail including Caddy module capability flags.

patch.description: Admin-only. Updates node name and/or enabled state.

delete.description: Admin-only. Deletes the node. Fails with 409 if the node has active routes.

get.description: Admin-only. Returns all customer services across all clients.

delete.description: Admin-only. Deletes the service. Fails with 409 if the service has active routes.

post.description: Admin-only. Supports Idempotency-Key header for safe retries.

parameters.name: Idempotency-Key
parameters.description: Optional client-generated key for idempotent POST. Same key within 24 h replays the stored response.

delete.description: Admin-only. Fails with 409 if the pool is referenced by a plan or node.

patch.description: Admin-only. All fields are optional - only provided fields are updated.

rate_limit_rpm.description: Set to 0 to remove the cap.

delete.description: Admin-only. Fails with 409 if the plan is in use by any service.

post.description: Admin-only. Creates a users row (role=client) and a clients row in one transaction. Supports Idempotency-Key.

password.description: Initial login password - at least 12 chars.

patch.description: Admin-only. Updates display name, email, and/or external_ref. All fields optional.

delete.description: Admin-only. Deletes user + client rows. Fails with 409 if the client has active services.
